name: MobileNetV3 Small
description: Ultra-lightweight MobileNet variant found through neural architecture search. Ideal for extreme mobile constraints with very small parameter count.

educationalNote.summary: Retinal layer receiving raw RGB pixel matrices.
educationalNote.detailed: Accepts normalized three-channel RGB images of size 224x224. This spatial standardization ensures weight compatibility in subsequent layers.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Establishes spatial dimensions for the network.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Fixed input sizes are critical to prevent spatial alignment errors.

calculationSteps.label: Kernel Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 16 filters convolving over 3 RGB channels with stride 2.

educationalNote.detailed: Extracts initial features while halving resolution to 112x112.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Reduces computational demands in initial stages.

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)
calculationSteps.explanation: Scale parameter (gamma) and shift parameter (beta) for active channel scaling.

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

educationalNote.detailed: Balances activation statistics before activation.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Enables faster and more robust gradient updates.

educationalNote.detailed: Hard-swish approximates swish activation: x * relu6(x+3)/6, which is faster on mobile platforms.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Combines non-linearity of ReLU with smooth curves of Swish to enhance gradient flows.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Approximates swish activation for fast mobile processing.

calculationSteps.label: Depthwise weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Kernel weights convolving over spatial maps.

educationalNote.detailed: Applies independent kernels over each channel to model spatial details.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Enables cheap feature extraction over large dimensions.

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)
calculationSteps.explanation: Scale parameter (gamma) and shift parameter (beta) for active channel scaling.

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

educationalNote.detailed: Collapses spatial dimensions to form channel global context vectors.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Used to model interdependencies between channels.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Collapses spatial details to channel context.

educationalNote.detailed: Compresses channel features to 8 with bias.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Controls SE param size.

educationalNote.summary: SE expand layer with hard-sigmoid activation.
educationalNote.detailed: Expands bottleneck back to output scales.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Gives weights representing importance of channels.

educationalNote.detailed: Scales the channels of feature maps by multiplying them with the computed excitation weights.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Dynamically selects relevant feature mappings.

calculationSteps.label: Project weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 1x1 projection compress weights.

educationalNote.detailed: Compresses channel volume to output channels 16 using a linear layer.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Preserves information manifolds in lower dimensions.

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)
calculationSteps.explanation: Scale parameter (gamma) and shift parameter (beta) for active channel scaling.

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

calculationSteps.label: Expand weights
calculationSteps.explanation: 1x1 expansion bottleneck conv.

educationalNote.detailed: Projects channels from 16 to a wider space of 72.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Expands representational capacity for the spatial depthwise conv.

calculationSteps.label: Conv weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Final feature synthesizer weights.

educationalNote.detailed: Synthesizes final block features before global pooling.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Controls feature parameters.

calculationSteps.label: Trainable Parameters (gamma, beta)
calculationSteps.explanation: Scale parameter (gamma) and shift parameter (beta) for active channel scaling.

calculationSteps.label: Non-trainable Parameters (mean, variance)
calculationSteps.explanation: Running mean and running variance tracked during training to normalize inference inputs.

educationalNote.detailed: Averages spatial dimensions to form a global feature vector.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Saves compute parameter sizes.
educationalNote.keyTakeaway: Replaces heavy fully connected layers with zero parameters.

calculationSteps.label: Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Bottleneck dense scaling weights.

educationalNote.detailed: Maps pooled visual representations into a smaller feature space before final classifications.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Protects and structures feature dimensions.

calculationSteps.label: Weights
calculationSteps.explanation: Fully connected mapping weights.

educationalNote.summary: Computes probabilities for the 1,000 target classes.
educationalNote.detailed: Uses softmax activation function to compute class probabilities.
educationalNote.whyItMatters: Converts numerical logits into values that sum to 1.

groups.name: Bottleneck Block 4
groups.description: Inverted bottleneck: 1x1 expansion → 5x5 dwconv → SE → 1x1 projection.

groups.name: Bottleneck Block 5
groups.description: Inverted bottleneck: 1x1 expansion → 5x5 dwconv → SE → 1x1 projection.

groups.name: Bottleneck Block 6
groups.description: Inverted bottleneck: 1x1 expansion → 5x5 dwconv → SE → 1x1 projection.

groups.name: Bottleneck Block 7
groups.description: Inverted bottleneck: 1x1 expansion → 5x5 dwconv → SE → 1x1 projection.

groups.name: Bottleneck Block 8
groups.description: Inverted bottleneck: 1x1 expansion → 5x5 dwconv → SE → 1x1 projection.

groups.name: Bottleneck Block 9
groups.description: Inverted bottleneck: 1x1 expansion → 5x5 dwconv → SE → 1x1 projection.

groups.name: Bottleneck Block 10
groups.description: Inverted bottleneck: 1x1 expansion → 5x5 dwconv → SE → 1x1 projection.

groups.name: Bottleneck Block 11
groups.description: Inverted bottleneck: 1x1 expansion → 5x5 dwconv → SE → 1x1 projection.

groups.name: Global Pooling & Classification
groups.description: Global average pooling followed by double dense classifier layers.
